title: helianthemum viscarium subsp. hispidulum
intro: Helianthemum viscarium subsp. hispidulum, a lesser-known subspecies of the rockrose family, is a flowering plant characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Helianthemum viscarium subsp. hispidulum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
